label: Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music

program.label: Solo for Mingus ("Myself When I Am Real")
program.work: Solo for Mingus ("Myself When I Am Real")

performers.name: Three or Four Shades of Blues

quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
quotes.text: You can hear an orchestra doing this. Carter brought this tune in called “Rise from Atlantis.” It had the same effect on me. I could hear the horns. I could hear the strings. I could hear the percussion. I mean it was an awesome tune. So Carter, I says, this was well worth the wait, you know, for your not contributing any music in this band. And we had a couple of anecdotes with Woody that were priceless. Because we were asked to do a tribute to Mingus when Mingus passed away. At Carnegie Hall. And of course the traditional thing, when you pay homage to a fallen soldier, you play his music. Well at the rehearsal Woody says, “I ain’t playing no Mingus music.” We say “Woody” — you know my brain is going man— He says “if they don’t want to hear my music that’s too bad. I’m playing my music.” That’s not the point, we’re supposed to — so what happened, I had to think quick. Because I’m part of this band, so whatever he does, I’m part of it. So I said well I’ve got to do something. I’d written this tune, which served both purposes. It helped keep him doing his band music, yet still pay homage to Mingus. I’d written this tune called “You Never Said You Had Go.” And he presented it in that context, as to why I’d written this music, just for Mingus. I remember — my wife doesn’t remember this but I talked about it and she was at the concert. She said she started crying for the sentiment of the tune. Because Mingus died kind of suddenly. So that’s why I said “You Never Said you Had to Go.” So I was like wheew, man, to get out of that one. Because at the rehearsal I was like oh sh— I can’t say that word right. And we got through it, we got through that. But you know more recently I’ve really been trying to just enjoy all the music, and enjoy everything that’s out there.
quotes.caption: Onaje Allan Gumbs recalls the 1979 Mingus tribute concert
quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
